title: ReconstructionManifest
description: The Manifest-style reconstruction info for lossy/holographic (VSA) forms, referenced from Meta.reconstruction (RFC-0001 §4.3 ReconInfo; specified in RFC-0003 §6). Explicitly distinguishes INDEXED RETRIEVAL (codebook + similarity + threshold; returns a stored atom; bounded-lossy; NOT holographic) from TRUE COMPOSITIONAL RECONSTRUCTION (requires the structural recipe/role schema + algebraic inverse ops; can recover novel combinations never stored) — VSA's defining capability over a hash table.

x-mycelium.$comment: Factorization (resonator networks) is Phase-3 exploratory and probabilistic-only (FR-C2); when present, `decode` carries the factor structure + iteration budget and `bound.strength` MUST NOT exceed Empirical.

mode.description: Which capability this manifest supports (RFC-0003 §6). CompositionalReconstruction MUST supply a `recipe`.

codebooks.description: Atomic item-memory codebook(s) as CONTENT-ADDRESSED references (Unison identity fits).

recipe.description: The compositional recipe / role schema: which ops combined which slots. REQUIRED (non-null) for CompositionalReconstruction; absent/null for IndexedRetrieval. A6-06: both `roles` and `structure` are REQUIRED, and `structure` maps each role name to the CONTENT-ADDRESSED hash of its role atom — matching the Rust `Recipe { roles, structure: Map<String, ContentHash> }` contract, so a schema-valid recipe is also kernel-valid.

structure.description: Role name → content hash of the role atom (an inspectable, content-addressed object).

decode.description: Decoding procedure + params: a cleanup threshold (indexed) or a resonator factor structure + iteration budget (compositional).

bound.description: The {ε, δ, strength} bound certificate for the reconstruction.

allOf.$comment: A6-06: the Cleanup decode procedure REQUIRES `cleanup_threshold` — matching the Rust contract (ReconInfo::new rejects a Cleanup spec with no threshold). `cleanup_threshold`'s [0,1] range is enforced unconditionally by the `decode.cleanup_threshold` property, so a stray threshold on any procedure (e.g. Resonator) is range-checked too.
